name: Puerto de la Virgen, Albánchez y Lubrín — circular through the Sierra de de los Filabres
name_original: Puerto de la Virgen, Albánchez y Lubrín — circular por la Sierra de de los Filabres
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Uleila Del Campo are situated in the foothills of the Sierra de los Filabres, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features quiet roads that wind through agricultural landscapes of vineyards, almond trees, and olive groves. Cyclists encounter challenging climbs and scenic routes, with elevations reaching over 1,500 meters. The area provides a diverse environment for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Uleila Del Campo The most popular road cycling route is Puerto de la Virgen, Albánchez y Lubrín — circular through the Sierra de de los Filabres, a 44.5 miles (71.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra de los Filabres. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de la Virgen – Puerto de la Virgen South Side loop from El Pilar, a difficult 47.9 miles (77.1 km) path. This route includes substantial climbing and offers expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Ascent to Collado Garcia — circular via Alcudia de Monteagud and Tahal, a 21.7 miles (35.0 km) trail leading through the foothills and villages, often completed in about 2 hours 3 minutes. Road cycling around Uleila Del Campo is defined by challenging climbs, quiet roads, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Uleila Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 290 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Uleila Del Campo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Uleila Del Campo?
A: Road cycling routes in Uleila Del Campo are characterized by varied terrain, ranging from challenging climbs in the Sierra de los Filabres to scenic roads winding through agricultural landscapes of vineyards, almond trees, and olive groves. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, with some routes reaching over 1,500 meters.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the area?
A: Yes, Uleila Del Campo is known for its challenging climbs. The region features the notable "Puerto de la Virgen," a 10-kilometer climb with an average gradient of 4%, and the "Alto Benizalon," a 5-kilometer climb with a 7% gradient. These ascents offer a rewarding experience on quiet, high-quality roads, making the area popular even for professional training.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Uleila Del Campo?
A: The region offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. During January and February, the countryside transforms with the vibrant bloom of almond trees, creating stunning landscapes. This period, along with other milder months, provides a picturesque backdrop for your rides. The area is particularly appealing during winter months when the almond blossoms are in full effect.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate road cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: While many routes feature significant climbs, there are options for different ability levels. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987666676/ascent-to-collado-garcia-circular-via-alcudia-de-monteagud-and-tahal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Collado Garcia — circular via Alcudia de Monteagud and Tahal&lt;/a&gt; is rated as moderate, offering a good option for those looking for a less strenuous but still engaging ride through the foothills and villages.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through the foothills of the Sierra de los Filabres, offering expansive views. You can also spot agricultural fields with vineyards, almond trees, and olive groves. Notable landmarks include the hill of Monteagud, topped by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4995439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de la Cabeza de Monteagud Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, which provides panoramic inland views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tahal Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2596408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Líjar Castle&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby points of interest.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987199365/puerto-de-la-virgen-albanchez-y-lubrin-circular-through-the-sierra-de-de-los-filabres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Virgen, Albánchez y Lubrín — circular through the Sierra de de los Filabres&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging loop through the mountains. Another is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Virgen – Puerto de la Virgen South Side loop from El Pilar&lt;/a&gt;, providing substantial climbing and views.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Uleila Del Campo?
A: The road cycling routes around Uleila Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the superb quality of the roads, the minimal traffic, and the diverse, scenic landscapes that make for a peaceful and picturesque riding experience.

Q: Are there any nearby towns or villages worth visiting during a cycling trip?
A: Absolutely. The routes often pass through quaint villages like Benizalón and Benitaglía. The nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3589750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sorbas&lt;/a&gt; is also a short distance away and is known for its remarkable gypsum caves. Additionally, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lubrín Village&lt;/a&gt; offers historical charm.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Uleila Del Campo area?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Uleila Del Campo, offering a wide selection for cyclists. These routes vary in difficulty, with 11 moderate and 9 difficult options, ensuring there's something for different skill levels.

Q: Where can I find more information about Uleila Del Campo beyond cycling?
A: For broader information about Uleila Del Campo, including its culture and other attractions, you can visit the local tourism guide at &lt;a href="https://visitingalmeria.com/towns-villages/uleila-del-campo-guide-discover/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitingalmeria.com&lt;/a&gt;. This can provide context for your visit beyond the cycling routes.

Q: Are there any routes that feature the famous Puerto de la Virgen climb?
A: Yes, the Puerto de la Virgen is a prominent feature in several routes. You can experience this well-known 10-kilometer climb on routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987199365/puerto-de-la-virgen-albanchez-y-lubrin-circular-through-the-sierra-de-de-los-filabres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Virgen, Albánchez y Lubrín — circular through the Sierra de de los Filabres&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Virgen – Puerto de la Virgen South Side loop from El Pilar&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: On the Peñón de Cóbdar (989 m) you can see one of the famous Spanish marble quarries. The mountain is right next to the white village of Cóbdar.
Albanchez is a locality in the province of Almeria, located in the region of Medio Almanzora, in the northern foothills of the Sierra de Los Filabres and to the west of the Sierra de Damián.
It is a small town located at the eastern end of the Sierra de los Filabres. Half of the town is located on a pair of rocky outcrops that elevate the houses. The church dominates the rest.

waypoints.name: Puerto de la Virgen South Side
